title: Fix a Broken Service Selector
description:
## Fix a Broken Service Selector

A service `my-svc` has been created but no traffic reaches the pods. The pods are labelled `app=backend`, but the service selector is misconfigured.

**Your task:**

Fix the `my-svc` service selector so it correctly targets the `backend` pods.

```bash
# Investigate:
kubectl describe service my-svc
kubectl get endpoints my-svc
kubectl get pods --show-labels
```

hints.title: Identify the mismatch
hints.body: Check what label the pods actually carry, then compare it against the service selector.
hints.command: kubectl get pods --show-labels && kubectl get service my-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector}'

hints.title: Patch the service selector
hints.body: The pods carry `app=backend` but the service selector says `app=wrong-label`. Use `kubectl patch` to point the selector at the correct label.

hints.title: Verify endpoints are populated
hints.body: After fixing the selector, Kubernetes will automatically update the endpoint slice. Confirm the service now has live pod addresses.

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: my-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: wrong-label
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
EOF

commands.command: kubectl get endpoints my-svc -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses[0].ip}' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' || echo empty
